name: gatehouse
displayName: GATEHOUSE — Define your repo's welcome experience
description: The ARRIVAL layer of the idea-to-production marketplace. GATEHOUSE lets a repository greet whoever opens it: when a project ships a maintainer-authored .claude/welcome.md, a SessionStart hook injects it so the agent can open with a one-line greeting and a conversational decision tree of the handful of things people come there to do — operate the software, or evolve it — and route the user straight to the right command, runbook, or downstream plugin. Smart-gated: it greets only on a vague/cold open and steps aside the moment the user states a concrete task, so it never nags. /gatehouse:define-welcome is the authoring tool — it reads a repo and writes its welcome experience for you. The engine ships in the marketplace; the content stays repo-local, exactly like CLAUDE.md. Stands alone; lights up ideator and foundry as the 'evolve the software' destinations when they are installed.

metadata.note: skills/, commands/, and hooks/hooks.json are auto-discovered from the plugin root. /gatehouse:define-welcome authors a repo's .claude/welcome.md. The SessionStart hook (hooks/inject-welcome.sh) renders that file when present and is a silent no-op when absent — safe in every repo. Carries the byte-identical SOUL.md + inject-soul.sh canonical copies (CI verify-prereqs.sh checks E/F).
